title: ODS Application Schema
description: Defines the structure for a dynamic application rendered from a JSON definition.

appName.description: The display name of the application.

startPage.description: The ID of the page to load when the app starts.

auth.description: RESERVED FOR FUTURE USE: Defines authentication requirements for the app.

pages.description: A dictionary of all pages available in the application.

dataSources.description: A dictionary of all data sources used by the application. Each key is a unique identifier referenced by components and actions.

dataSource.title: DataSource Object
dataSource.description: Defines a data endpoint. Use 'local://<tableName>' for framework-managed local storage, or a full URL (https://) for external REST APIs.

url.description: The data endpoint. Use 'local://<tableName>' for local storage managed by the framework, or a full URL for an external API.

method.description: The access method. For local:// sources, GET reads all rows and POST inserts a new row. For external URLs, this is the HTTP method.

fields.description: Optional. Explicitly defines the columns/fields for a local:// data source. If omitted for a local:// source, the schema is auto-inferred from the first form that submits to it.

seedData.description: Optional. An array of objects to pre-populate a local:// data source when the table is first created. Each object's keys must match the field names.

fieldDefinition.title: Field Definition
fieldDefinition.description: Defines a single field/column in a data source or form.

name.description: The programmatic name of the field, used as the column name in storage.

type.description: The data type of the field. Used for input validation and display formatting.

label.description: Optional. A human-readable label for the field.

title.description: The title displayed for the page.

content.description: An array of components to render on the page.

dataSource.description: The ID of the dataSource to read data from.

id.description: A unique identifier for the form.

action.title: Action Object
action.description: Defines an action to be performed, like navigate or submit.

target.description: The ID of the page to navigate to.

withData.description: An optional object of data to pass to the target page.

dataSource.description: The ID of the dataSource to submit to.

target.description: The ID of the form to submit.

styleHint.title: StyleHint Object
styleHint.description: An optional object providing abstract styling guidance. Frameworks should interpret known hints and gracefully ignore unknown ones.
